courses.description: The Bachelor of Arts (BA) distance program offers a flexible and comprehensive foundation in humanities and social sciences. It develops critical thinking, communication, and analytical skills across multiple disciplines, preparing students for careers in education, civil services, media, and further academic pursuits.
courses.eligibility: 10+2 or equivalent in any stream from a recognized board.

subjects.name: English Communication Skills
subjects.description: Fundamentals of grammar, vocabulary building, and written communication.

subjects.name: Introduction to Political Theory
subjects.description: Basic concepts of political science and key ideologies.

subjects.name: History of India (Ancient Period)
subjects.description: Study of Indian history from prehistoric times to 1200 AD.

subjects.name: Introduction to Sociology
subjects.description: Core sociological concepts and social institutions.

subjects.name: Microeconomics
subjects.description: Principles of individual economic units and market mechanisms.

subjects.name: Punjabi Literature (Prose)
subjects.description: Study of prominent Punjabi prose writers and essays.

subjects.name: Modern Indian History
subjects.description: India's struggle for independence and post-independence consolidation.

subjects.name: Social Research Methods
subjects.description: Introduction to qualitative and quantitative research in social sciences.

subjects.name: Macroeconomics
subjects.description: Analysis of economy-wide phenomena and policy implications.

subjects.name: Contemporary Political Issues
subjects.description: Examination of current national and international political developments.

subjects.name: Sociology of Change and Development
subjects.description: Theories and patterns of social change with special reference to India.

subjects.name: Project Work / Dissertation
subjects.description: Independent research on a chosen topic under faculty guidance.

courses.description: The Master of Arts (MA) distance program provides advanced knowledge and research orientation in specialized disciplines. It is designed for graduates seeking expertise in humanities and social sciences, leading to careers in academia, research, public policy, and senior administrative roles.
courses.eligibility: Bachelor's degree in any discipline from a recognized university.

subjects.name: Literary Criticism and Theory
subjects.description: Major critical approaches from classical to contemporary times.

subjects.name: Advanced Political Theory
subjects.description: In-depth analysis of key political concepts and ideologies.

subjects.name: Research Methodology
subjects.description: Advanced tools and techniques for social science research.

subjects.name: Modern Indian Political Thought
subjects.description: Political ideas of leading Indian thinkers.

subjects.name: Modern World History
subjects.description: Global historical transformations from 1789 to 1945.

subjects.name: Public Administration: Principles and Practices
subjects.description: Theoretical foundations and functioning of administrative systems.

subjects.name: Indian English Literature
subjects.description: Study of major Indian authors writing in English.

subjects.name: Dissertation
subjects.description: Original research work culminating in a thesis submission.

courses.description: The Bachelor of Library and Information Science (B.Lib.I.Sc) is a one-year professional program that equips students with the skills required to manage libraries and information centers effectively. The curriculum covers classification, cataloguing, and digital library management, preparing graduates for roles in academic, public, and special libraries.

subjects.name: Library and Society
subjects.description: Foundations of librarianship and role of libraries in society.

subjects.name: Library Classification (Theory)
subjects.description: Principles and schemes of document classification.

subjects.name: Library Cataloguing (Theory)
subjects.description: Standards and rules for bibliographic description.

subjects.name: Information Sources and Services
subjects.description: Reference sources and user services in libraries.

subjects.name: Library Management
subjects.description: Principles of administration applied to libraries.

subjects.name: Digital Libraries and IT Applications
subjects.description: Introduction to digital library software and services.

courses.description: The Master of Library and Information Science (M.Lib.I.Sc) is an advanced program focusing on research methodology, ICT applications in libraries, and knowledge management. It prepares professionals for leadership roles in managing modern information systems and digital repositories.
courses.eligibility: Bachelor of Library and Information Science (B.Lib.I.Sc).

subjects.name: Information Technology in Libraries (Advanced)
subjects.description: Deep dive into library automation software and networking.

subjects.name: Research Methodology and Statistical Techniques
subjects.description: Quantitative and qualitative research methods for LIS.

subjects.name: Knowledge Management
subjects.description: Strategies for capturing, sharing, and using organizational knowledge.

subjects.name: Academic Library Systems and Services
subjects.description: Specialized management of college and university libraries.

courses.description: The Bachelor of Computer Applications (BCA) distance program provides a strong foundation in computer science and applications. It covers programming languages, database management, networking, and web development, preparing students for careers in the IT industry or higher studies like MCA.
courses.eligibility: 10+2 with Mathematics/Computer Science/Information Practices or equivalent.

subjects.name: Fundamentals of Computers and IT
subjects.description: Basic concepts of hardware, software, and operating systems.

subjects.name: Programming in C
subjects.description: Procedural programming fundamentals using C language.

subjects.name: Data Structures using C
subjects.description: Organization and manipulation of data efficiently.

subjects.name: Database Management System
subjects.description: Relational database design and SQL queries.

subjects.name: Operating System
subjects.description: Core functions and architecture of modern operating systems.

subjects.name: Object Oriented Programming with C++
subjects.description: Introduction to OOP concepts using C++.

subjects.name: Computer Networks
subjects.description: Principles of data communication and network protocols.

subjects.name: Web Development
subjects.description: Creating interactive and responsive websites.

subjects.name: Java Programming
subjects.description: Platform independent application development.

subjects.name: Software Engineering
subjects.description: Methodologies for developing large-scale software systems.

subjects.name: Mobile Application Development
subjects.description: Introduction to building apps for Android platform.

subjects.name: Major Project
subjects.description: Comprehensive project implementing concepts learned throughout the program.

courses.description: The Master of Computer Applications (MCA) is a postgraduate program designed to meet the demand for qualified professionals in the IT field. The curriculum emphasizes advanced programming, cloud computing, AI, and cyber security, enabling graduates to pursue careers in software development, system architecture, and IT consulting.
courses.eligibility: BCA / B.Sc (CS/IT) / Bachelor's degree with Mathematics at 10+2 or Graduation level.

subjects.name: Advanced Java Programming
subjects.description: Enterprise application development using Java EE.

subjects.name: Data Warehousing and Data Mining
subjects.description: Techniques for analyzing large datasets.

subjects.name: Cloud Computing
subjects.description: Principles and practices of distributed computing on the cloud.

subjects.name: Machine Learning
subjects.description: Algorithms that allow computers to learn from data.

subjects.name: Cyber Security
subjects.description: Protection of internet-connected systems and data.

subjects.name: Big Data Analytics
subjects.description: Processing and analyzing massive volumes of data.

subjects.name: Project Work
subjects.description: Industry-oriented project or research dissertation.

courses.description: The Bachelor of Commerce (B.Com) distance program offers a comprehensive understanding of business, finance, and economics. It builds a strong foundation in accounting, taxation, and business law, preparing graduates for careers in banking, finance, corporate sectors, and professional courses like CA/CS.
courses.eligibility: 10+2 in Commerce stream or with Mathematics/Economics.

subjects.name: Financial Accounting
subjects.description: Principles and practices of recording business transactions.

subjects.name: Business Law
subjects.description: Legal framework governing business contracts and transactions.

subjects.name: Corporate Accounting
subjects.description: Accounting for joint stock companies.

subjects.name: Business Economics
subjects.description: Application of economic theory to business decisions.

subjects.name: Income Tax Law and Practice
subjects.description: Computation of taxable income and tax liability for individuals.

subjects.name: Cost Accounting
subjects.description: Determination and control of costs in manufacturing and services.

subjects.name: Auditing
subjects.description: Principles and procedures for examining financial records.

subjects.name: Financial Management
subjects.description: Management of funds and capital structure.

subjects.name: Goods and Services Tax (GST)
subjects.description: Indirect tax regime in India.

subjects.name: Management Accounting
subjects.description: Use of accounting information for managerial decision making.

subjects.name: E-Commerce
subjects.description: Business transactions conducted electronically.

subjects.name: Project Report / Viva Voce
subjects.description: Practical application of commerce concepts through a mini-project.

courses.description: The Master of Commerce (M.Com) distance program provides advanced knowledge in accounting, finance, and business strategy. It is ideal for graduates aiming for senior positions in corporate finance, research, or teaching careers in commerce and management.

subjects.name: Advanced Financial Accounting
subjects.description: Accounting for mergers, acquisitions, and consolidated financial statements.

subjects.name: Strategic Financial Management
subjects.description: High-level financial strategies for maximizing shareholder wealth.

subjects.name: International Business Environment
subjects.description: Dynamics of global trade and cross-border operations.

subjects.name: Research Methodology in Commerce
subjects.description: Advanced research tools for business and commerce.

subjects.name: Direct Tax Planning
subjects.description: Advanced tax planning strategies for individuals and corporates.

subjects.name: Security Analysis and Portfolio Management
subjects.description: Valuation of financial assets and construction of optimal portfolios.

subjects.name: Dissertation
subjects.description: In-depth research on a contemporary issue in commerce/finance.

courses.description: The Bachelor of Business Administration (BBA) distance program provides a broad understanding of business operations and management principles. It develops leadership, communication, and analytical skills essential for entry-level managerial roles and entrepreneurship.

subjects.name: Principles of Management
subjects.description: Foundational concepts of planning, organizing, staffing, directing, and controlling.

subjects.name: Business Communication
subjects.description: Effective written and oral communication in a corporate environment.

subjects.name: Organizational Behavior
subjects.description: Understanding individual and group dynamics in organizations.

subjects.name: Marketing Management
subjects.description: Core concepts of product, price, place, and promotion.

subjects.name: Human Resource Management
subjects.description: Processes involved in managing an organization's workforce.

subjects.name: Financial Management
subjects.description: Introduction to financial statement analysis and capital budgeting.

subjects.name: Entrepreneurship Development
subjects.description: Process of starting and managing a new business venture.

subjects.name: Business Research Methods
subjects.description: Tools and techniques for business decision making.

subjects.name: Operations Management
subjects.description: Design and control of production processes.

subjects.name: Business Ethics and Corporate Governance
subjects.description: Ethical issues in business and responsibilities of the board.

subjects.name: E-Commerce
subjects.description: Electronic business models and digital payments.

subjects.name: Project Work
subjects.description: Comprehensive project on a business problem or business plan.

courses.description: The Master of Business Administration (MBA) distance program is designed for working professionals and graduates aiming for leadership roles. The curriculum integrates strategic thinking, finance, marketing, and analytics, enabling students to drive organizational success in a globalized economy.
courses.eligibility: Bachelor's degree in any discipline. Work experience preferred.

subjects.name: Strategic Management
subjects.description: Formulation and implementation of long-term corporate strategy.

subjects.name: Advanced Financial Management
subjects.description: Corporate finance decisions and risk management.

subjects.name: Operations and Supply Chain Management
subjects.description: Optimizing processes from procurement to delivery.

subjects.name: Digital Marketing
subjects.description: Leveraging online channels for brand building and lead generation.

subjects.name: Business Analytics
subjects.description: Data-driven decision making using statistical tools.

subjects.name: Leadership and Change Management
subjects.description: Skills required to lead teams through organizational transformation.

subjects.name: Dissertation / Capstone Project
subjects.description: Application of MBA concepts to solve a real-world business problem.

courses.description: The Bachelor of Science in Information Technology (B.Sc IT) distance program focuses on the practical application of computing technology to solve organizational problems. It covers networking, databases, and web technologies, preparing students for technical support and system administration roles.

subjects.name: Programming Fundamentals

subjects.name: Database Management Systems

subjects.name: Software Project Management

It equips graduates with the technical expertise required for senior IT roles and research in the field of computing.

subjects.name: Information Security and Cyber Forensics
